title: ChainClosed payload
description: Emitted when a chain closes via chain_close. Per-task TaskCompleted events should all have already landed before this fires; the chain handler verifies this and refuses to close a chain with open tasks. Closure summary is the load-bearing 'what did we ship' record but is optional in the schema — the current chain_close handler accepts a nil summary for legacy callers; the schema captures whatever the handler supplies without retrofitting the historical permissive behavior.

closure_summary.description: What this chain shipped, in narrative form. The audit-trail of intent across the whole chain — distinct from per-task closure_summary which records each task's ship-state. The dashboard's chain-detail panel surfaces this as the headline. Null when the caller chain_closed without supplying a summary.
